title: Priority beats confidence
summary: The answer is the movement that obeys the rule, not the one that looks bold.
body: Correct answer: follow the stated order, not the strongest-looking car.
Why: right-of-way resolves the crossing before size or noise can.
Trap: priority inversion makes the biggest mover seem first.
Reclaim: restate the rule, then compare arrival and position.

title: Yielding is active, not timid
summary: A yield sign asks for deliberate slowing and a safe gap, not frozen hesitation.
body: Correct answer: slow and give way if the crossing needs it.
Why: yielding is an active decision to let safe flow happen.
Trap: false-yield makes you wait even when the rule allows you through.
Reclaim: check the gap, then move with purpose.

title: A stop sign means a full stop
summary: The stop must be complete before you scan and proceed.
body: Correct answer: make the stop complete before moving again.
Why: the pause resets your view of the crossing.
Trap: stop-sign bait turns a roll-through into a fake stop.
Reclaim: stop, scan, then re-enter the rule.

title: Signaling is not enough by itself
summary: The signal announces intent, but mirrors and blind-spot checks confirm safety.
body: Correct answer: signal, then check mirrors and blind spot before moving.
Why: other drivers need the cue, but you still need the clearance.
Trap: signal without check ignores the hidden lane threat.
Reclaim: announce the move and verify the space.

title: Pedestrians own the crossing
summary: Walkers and cyclists in the crossing force the driver to wait and protect the space.
body: Correct answer: stop and yield to the crossing person.
Why: the rule protects vulnerable road users first.
Trap: pedestrian ghost hides the person until it is too late.
Reclaim: slow early and scan the edges, not just the open lane.

title: A roundabout needs a real opening
summary: The vehicle should enter only when the circular flow leaves a safe gap.
body: Correct answer: wait for a safe gap before entering.
Why: the roundabout is moving traffic, not a parking lot.
Trap: roundabout confusion treats a dangerous slot like a safe one.
Reclaim: measure the flow, then join it.

title: Size is not priority
summary: A truck or loud vehicle may be intimidating, but the rule still decides the order.
body: Correct answer: size alone does not grant priority.
Why: right-of-way is a rule, not a popularity contest.
Trap: priority inversion rewards the wrong signal.
Reclaim: ask who has the rule, not who looks powerful.

title: Slow down when the field goes dark
summary: Low visibility demands earlier braking and more cautious scanning.
body: Correct answer: reduce speed and scan earlier.
Why: the safer pace keeps the road legible.
Trap: late brake keeps speed too long and shrinks the response window.
Reclaim: slow before the hazard arrives.

title: A merge needs a gap and matched speed
summary: The merge works when the opening and the speed both support the move.
body: Correct answer: find a real gap and match speed before entering.
Why: a merge is a timed fit, not a leap of faith.
Trap: gap hunger grabs openings that are not actually safe.
Reclaim: wait, match, then move.

title: A miss becomes a reclaim loop
summary: The recovery path is to name the trap, restate the rule, and try again.
body: Correct answer: use the miss as a rule recall, not a dead end.
Why: the game should turn a mistake into a sharper memory.
Trap: the miss itself can become the next trap if you do not name it.
Reclaim: trap name, rule, retry.
